mitigations.mitigation_name: Deploy inline Snort IPS between FortiGate and internal switch to block ICMP flood
Create FortiGate egress/filter rules to block ICMP from the compromised agent while allowing HTTP/HTTPS (egress filtering on the firewall)

args.command:
config firewall address
edit "agent_192.168.1.3"
set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
next
end
config firewall policy
edit 1
set name "Deny_ICMP_from_agent"
set srcintf "any"
set dstintf "any"
set srcaddr "agent_192.168.1.3"
set dstaddr "all"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "PING"
set logtraffic all
next
edit 2
set name "Allow_HTTPs_from_agent"
set srcintf "any"
set dstintf "any"
set srcaddr "agent_192.168.1.3"
set dstaddr "all"
set action accept
set service "HTTPS" "HTTP"
set nat enable
set schedule "always"
set logtraffic all
next
end

mitigations.mitigation_name: Apply host-based ICMP rate-limiting and drop rules on the compromised Ubuntu agent using iptables (throttle allowed ICMP, drop the rest)

args.command: iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p icmp -m limit --limit 1/s --limit-burst 5 -j ACCEPT; iptables -A OUTPUT -p icmp -j DROP; iptables -L OUTPUT -n --line-numbers
